popularStory.headline: Jeanne Ière de Navarre
popularStory.description: Jeanne Ire de Navarre (née le 14 janvier 1273 à Bar-sur-Seine, Champagne - morte le 2 avril 1305 à Vincennes, France), princesse de la maison de Champagne, fut reine de Navarre de 1274 à 1305 et reine …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France於1273年1月14日出生在法國哈帕格-亞登奧布巴-素-時，她的父親Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre29歲，母親Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre26歲。她於1284年8月16日與Philip IV Roi de France在西班牙納法洛亞易盧納結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1305年4月2日在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內文斯享年32歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France于1273年1月14日出生在法国哈帕格-亞登奧布巴-素-时，她的父亲Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre29岁，母亲Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre26岁。她于1284年8月16日与Philip IV Roi de France在西班牙納法洛亞易盧納结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1305年4月2日在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瓦爾-德-馬內文斯享年32岁，埋葬在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Navarre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Jacques, Anatole, Angelle, Camille, Marcelle.
French: habitational name from Navarre (see   Navarra  ).

variants.name: Navarra
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Edgardo, Guido, Santo, Vito, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonio, Benedicto, Camillo, Dante, Emilio, Fausto, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Mario, Romulo, Sergio.  Spanish Agustin, Herlinda, Manuel, Solita.

Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from Navarre (Castilian Navarra, Basque Nafarroa, French Navarre), a largely Basque-speaking region now divided between Spain and France, but in the Middle Ages an independent Basque kingdom. The name of the region derives from Basque naba ‘plain next to mountains’. Compare Navarre .

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France was born on 14 January 1273, in Bar-sur-Seine, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, her father, Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre, was 29 and her mother, Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre, was 26. She married Philip IV Roi de France on 16 August 1284, in Iruña, Nafarroa, Spain. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 2 April 1305, in Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 32, and was buried in Paris, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France est née le 14 janvier 1273, à Bar-sur-Seine, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, France, son père, Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Philip IV Roi de France le 16 août 1284, à Iruña, Nafarroa, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 2 avril 1305, à Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterrée à Paris, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France am 14. Januar 1273 in Bar-sur-Seine, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre 29 und ihre Mutter Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre 26. Sie hat am 16. August 1284 in Iruña, Nafarroa, Spanien Philip IV Roi de France geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 2. April 1305 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Frankreich und wurde in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France è nata il il 14 gennaio 1273 nel Bar-sur-Seine, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, sua padre, Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Philip IV Roi de France il 16 agosto 1284, nel Iruña, Nafarroa, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 2 aprile 1305, nel Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolta a Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de Franceが1273年1月14日にフランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンアウベバー-スル-セアインで生まれた時，父Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarreは29歳，母Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarreは26歳であった。1284年8月16日にスペインナファロアイルnアでPhilip IV Roi de Franceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1305年4月2日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスヴァル-デ-マルンヴィンセンスで32歳で死去し，フランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France(이)가 1273년 1월 14일 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 오브 바-수-세이인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1284년 8월 16일 스페인 나파로 이루n아에서 Philip IV Roi de France(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1305년 4월 2일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 발-데-마니 빈스네스에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에 묻혔다.

mn.14 January 1273–2 April 1305: 1273 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 14–1305 оны Дөрөвдүгээр сарын 2

pt.14 January 1273–2 April 1305: 14 de janeiro de 1273–2 de abril de 1305
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France nasceu em 14 de janeiro de 1273, em Bar-sur-Seine, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, França, seu pai, Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Philip IV Roi de France em 16 de agosto de 1284, em Iruña, Nafarroa, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 2 de abril de 1305, em Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, França, com 32 anos, e foi sepultada em Paris, Île-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France родилась 14 января 1273 г. в местности бар-сур-син, ауб, чампагне-арденн, Франция, её отцу по имени Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip IV Roi de France 16 августа 1284 г. в местности ируnа, нафарроа, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 2 апреля 1305 г. в местности винценнесь, вал-де-марн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 32 лет и была похоронена в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.14 January 1273–2 April 1305: 14 de enero de 1273–2 de abril de 1305
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne I de Navarre Reine de France nació el 14 de enero de 1273, en Bar-sur-Seine, Aube, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia, su papá, Henri I de Navarre Roi de Navarre, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Blanche d’Artois Reine de Navarre, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Philip IV Roi de France el 16 de agosto de 1284, en Pamplona, Navarra, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 2 de abril de 1305, en Vincennes, Val-de-Marne, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultada en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia.
